heroDesc: Fast and accurate sheet profile cutting
bodyTitle: Laser Cutting for fast metal sheet profiling
bodyDesc: Laser cutting is suitable for 2D profiles, holes and slots in metal sheet. It provides fast cutting and consistent contours, and is often combined with bending, inserts, welding and finishing. Actual edge quality depends on material, thickness, assist gas, heat input and hole-size ratio.

panels.title: Efficiency
panels.subtitle: Fast Nesting
panels.body: Good for varied sheet profiles and small to medium batches.

panels.title: Quality Focus
panels.subtitle: Heat / Burr
panels.body: Thick plate and small holes require attention to taper, dross and heat distortion.

panels.title: Process Pairing
panels.subtitle: Bend / Weld
panels.body: Can continue to bending, inserts, welding and coating after cutting.

tableRows: Capability depends on material and machine power
Thick stock and long edges may vary more
Small holes may be affected by piercing
Sharp edge or chamfer should be specified

steps.title: Drawing and material check
steps.body: Confirm material, thickness, grain direction, hole size and cosmetic face.

steps.title: Nesting and path planning
steps.body: Optimize nesting, lead-in points and heat distortion control.

steps.title: Cutting and deburring
steps.body: Cut profiles and remove dross and sharp edges.

steps.title: Bending or finishing
steps.body: Continue to bending, welding, coating or packaging as required.

asideCta.title: Need a Manufacturing Plan?
asideCta.body: After drawings are submitted, engineers can review geometry, material and volume to suggest a suitable process route.

related.title: Related Process Recommendations
related.desc: Depending on geometry, material and delivery stage, other processes can be combined into a complete manufacturing flow.

items.title: Sheet Metal Fabrication
items.body: Cut blanks can be bent into structural parts.

faq.title: Laser Cutting FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Laser Cutting suitable for?
items.a: Laser Cutting is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Laser Cutting quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Laser Cutting?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
